profile_table.Predictability: Fast but more variable than predictability-first alternatives
profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Distributed overhead profile; optimize entry/setup and execution path before residual tail
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Generally usable with healthier success profile; mainly push-associated

profile_table.Speed profile: Mixed-speed case: early entry can be competitive, but completion tail is heavy
profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Tail-heavy mixed case; reduce bespoke post-execution work and standardize orchestration
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Sparse and context-sensitive; low observed success and often pull_request-associated

profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Execution-centric profile; first optimization target is the main execution path
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Most usable and success-oriented outcomes; mainly schedule-associated

profile_table.Speed profile: Slow sustained-execution profile with heavy entry burden
profile_table.Overhead source & lever: Heavy entry plus heavy execution; optimize provisioning delay and provider-side execution cost
profile_table.Verdict & deployment: Usable verdicts common, but success strongly trigger-conditioned; mainly schedule-associated

observations.obs_title: Community is the fastest overall operational profile
observations.question: Which style is the fastest overall operational profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=1.37e-63, epsilon^2=0.038; vs GMD: p_adj=1.9e-11, rbc=0.244, vs Third-Party: p_adj=2.57e-54, rbc=0.406, vs Custom: p_adj=0.00214, rbc=0.284. stored='Community', winner='Community'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: study_run_duration_seconds; winner = lowest median run duration.

observations.obs_title: GMD reaches the instrumentation path fastest, but does not finish fastest
observations.question: Which style shows the clearest fast-entry profile without being the fastest overall finisher?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on study_pre_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds: p=1.49e-10, epsilon^2=0.010; vs Community: p_adj=0.000334, rbc=0.194, vs Third-Party: p_adj=2.08e-33, rbc=0.954, vs Custom: p_adj=0.0784, rbc=0.173. stored='GMD', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: study_pre_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds; winner = lowest median fast-entry metric.

observations.obs_title: Third-Party is the slowest sustained-execution profile
observations.question: Which style is the slowest sustained-execution profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on study_invocation_execution_window_selected_stage3_seconds: p=3.28e-38, epsilon^2=0.038; vs Community: p_adj=1.38e-25, rbc=0.616, vs GMD: p_adj=3.11e-09, rbc=0.468, vs Custom: p_adj=2.49e-08, rbc=0.677. stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: study_invocation_execution_window_selected_stage3_seconds; winner = highest median sustained-execution burden.

observations.obs_title: Custom shows a mixed speed profile
observations.question: Which style shows a mixed speed profile with competitive entry, middling core path, and a long completion tail?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on study_post_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds: p=7.14e-60, epsilon^2=0.060; vs Custom: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.827, vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.731, vs Third-Party: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.012. stored='GMD', winner='Custom'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: study_post_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds; winner = highest median completion-tail metric.

observations.obs_title: Community’s speed advantage is concentrated in the core execution windows and comes with a longer tail
observations.question: Which style combines a fast core execution profile with a longer residual tail?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on study_post_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds: p=1.43e-59, epsilon^2=0.059; vs Community: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.731, vs Third-Party: p_adj=1, rbc=-0.012. stored='GMD', winner='Community'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: study_post_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds; winner = highest median tail metric within the fast-core candidate set.

observations.obs_title: GMD is the most predictable style on the main completion-oriented measures
observations.question: Which style is the most predictable on the main completion-oriented measures?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on predictability loss on study_run_duration_seconds: p=9.76e-111, epsilon^2=0.066; vs Community: p_adj=2.71e-100, rbc=0.772, vs Third-Party: p_adj=7.95e-48, rbc=0.634, vs Custom: p_adj=1.07e-10, rbc=0.669. stored='GMD', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: predictability loss on study_run_duration_seconds; winner = lowest median normalized deviation.

observations.obs_title: Community is fast in typical terms, but predictability-poor
observations.question: Which style is fast in typical terms but predictability-poor?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on predictability loss on study_run_duration_seconds: p=1.81e-100, epsilon^2=0.063; vs GMD: p_adj=9.05e-101, rbc=0.772. stored='Community', winner='Community'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: predictability loss on study_run_duration_seconds within the two fastest styles by run duration; winner = highest median normalized deviation.

observations.obs_title: Third-Party carries the strongest absolute tail-risk profile
observations.question: Which style carries the strongest absolute tail-risk profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on study_run_duration_seconds: p=1.37e-63, epsilon^2=0.038; vs Community: p_adj=1.71e-54, rbc=0.406, vs GMD: p_adj=1.37e-60, rbc=0.717, vs Custom: p_adj=0.0747, rbc=0.147. stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: upper-tail burden on study_run_duration_seconds; winner = highest P90.

observations.obs_title: Custom has a mixed predictability profile and should be interpreted cautiously
observations.question: Which style shows a mixed predictability profile that should be interpreted cautiously?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on predictability loss on study_pre_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds: p=3.51e-77, epsilon^2=0.077; vs Community: p_adj=0.997, rbc=-0.299, vs GMD: p_adj=3.17e-10, rbc=0.774, vs Third-Party: p_adj=5.25e-07, rbc=0.623. stored='Custom', winner='Community'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: predictability loss on study_pre_invocation_selected_stage3_seconds; winner = highest median normalized deviation.

observations.obs_title: GMD is the clearest execution-centric style
observations.question: Which style is the clearest execution-centric overhead profile?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on execution_window_share: p=1.97e-43, epsilon^2=0.043; vs Community: p_adj=2.08e-39, rbc=0.712, vs Third-Party: p_adj=1.06e-24, rbc=0.811, vs Custom: p_adj=5.01e-14, rbc=0.907. stored='GMD', winner='GMD'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: execution_window_share; winner = highest median execution share.

observations.obs_title: Third-Party is best characterized by heavy entry and heavy execution, not by a dominant completion tail
observations.question: Which style is best characterized by heavy entry plus heavy execution rather than a dominant completion tail?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Kruskal on pre_invocation_share: p=3.47e-12, epsilon^2=0.012; vs Community: p_adj=9.16e-06, rbc=0.251, vs GMD: p_adj=2.55e-22, rbc=0.771, vs Custom: p_adj=8.1e-08, rbc=0.666. stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: pre_invocation_share; winner = highest median heavy-entry/heavy-execution proxy.

observations.obs_title: Community has a distributed overhead profile rather than a single dominant overhead source
observations.question: Which style has a distributed overhead profile rather than a single dominant overhead source?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: No usable values for validation metric 'max_phase_share'.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: max_phase_share; winner = lowest median maximum phase share.

observations.obs_title: Custom remains a cautious, tail-heavy mixed case

Fail only when winner changes with significant omnibus + pairwise support and meaningful effect size.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: post_invocation_share; winner = highest median post-invocation share.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: Styles differ in usable run-level verdict rates, although the overall separation is modest
observations.question: Which style currently has the strongest usable run-level verdict rate?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Chi-square for usable_verdict_rate: p=2.25e-15, Cramer's V=0.091; stored='GMD', winner='GMD'.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: usable verdict rate; winner = highest rate.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: Among usable verdicts, styles differ sharply in success rate
observations.question: Which style currently has the strongest success rate among usable verdicts?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Chi-square for success_among_usable: p=2.03e-247, Cramer's V=0.384; stored='GMD', winner='GMD'.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: success rate among usable verdicts; winner = highest rate.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: Styles are deployed in markedly different CI trigger contexts
observations.question: Do the styles remain deployed in markedly different CI trigger contexts?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Chi-square on style × event: p=0, Cramer's V=0.397; schedule-share winner='GMD', stored='Yes', top_gap=0.0288.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: schedule-triggered deployment share on first-attempt runs; winner = highest schedule share.

observations.rq_title: Deployment context and run-level verdict usability
observations.obs_title: For Third-Party and Custom, success rate is strongly trigger-conditioned
observations.question: Which styles remain strongly trigger-conditioned in success behavior?
observations.L1_note_2026_04_14: Trigger-conditioned spread gap=0.3200; stored='Third-Party', winner='Third-Party'. Primary measurement: trigger-conditioned success-rate spread; winner = largest spread.
observations.L1_favored_note_2026_04_14: Primary measurement: trigger-conditioned success-rate spread; winner = largest spread.
